payload.summary: The weekly review pulse reveals mixed user experiences with the app. While some users appreciate the app's features and smooth UI, others express frustration with the new design, technical issues, and poor customer support. The app's performance is a major concern, with users experiencing glitches, errors, and difficulties with transactions.
quotes: Great app but the new design is not that great. Old design gave a lot more information just at a glance.
Don't make a mistake of opening an account with this platform. They very conveniently opened account with SBM bank of Mauritius and now they say they are not associated with SBM bank.
Interface is complex and not good. you cannot increase font size. Even if you increase phone font or zoom setting, it will not reflect here.
action_items: Revamp the app's design to improve user experience and provide more intuitive features
Enhance customer support by providing timely and effective solutions to user complaints
Address technical issues and errors to ensure seamless transactions and improve overall app performance
action_ideas: Revamp the app's design to improve user experience and provide more intuitive features
Enhance customer support by providing timely and effective solutions to user complaints
Address technical issues and errors to ensure seamless transactions and improve overall app performance

explanation_bullets: A mutual fund exit load is a fee charged to investors when they redeem or withdraw their investments from a mutual fund.
The exit load is typically expressed as a percentage of the redeemed amount.
It is usually levied to discourage early withdrawals from the fund, especially during the initial years.
The exit load fee varies across mutual funds and can range from 0.5% to 3% or more, depending on the fund's policy.
The fee is deducted from the redemption proceeds, and the remaining amount is paid to the investor.
The exit load structure and rates are specified in the mutual fund's prospectus or offer document.
A brokerage fee is a charge levied by a brokerage firm for facilitating the purchase or sale of securities.
It is typically a percentage of the transaction amount or a flat fee per transaction.
Brokerage fees can vary depending on the type of investment, such as stocks, bonds, or mutual funds.
Some brokerage firms may offer discounted fees for frequent traders or high-volume investors.
Brokerage fees are usually deducted from the investor's account balance.
Fees can be affected by market conditions, regulatory requirements, and the brokerage firm's business model.

I have never added, authorized, or linked this account at any point. This appears to have been auto-linked by the system, which raises serious concerns about account security, data accuracy, and fund safety. I hope the support team resolves this matter urgently and transparently.

INDmoney offers useful features, but constant UI/UX changes make it frustrating to use. Frequent updates shift menus and navigation, forcing users to relearn the app again and again. Instead of improving usability, many changes create confusion and slow down simple tasks. A finance app should feel stable and predictable. Please focus on consistency and meaningful improvements rather than frequent redesigns.

The USD conversion via ICICI Bank is misleading. The exchange rate shown in the INDMoney app is not the rate actually applied—there’s a difference of about ₹0.30 per dollar, with the bank charging a higher rate. When I contacted ICICI Bank, they stated that since this is a third-party transaction, they cannot address the discrepancy. On the other hand, INDMoney’s support team was unable to provide a clear explanation for the difference in exchange rates.
